author: Marvin Scholz
message:
meson: Do not build test executables by default

Now they will only be built when actually needed, similar to how
autotools handles this.

Fix #83

author: Marvin Scholz
message: Update Changelog and version to 0.4.1

author: Marvin Scholz
message:
AmbisonicBinauralizer: Handle exceptions initializing HRTF

As a follow up to 5dda58ee0bee3ec808d01b044dcf7392b7d30c3d, handle
exceptions when initializing the HRTF object. Not only will this catch
the newly raised exception when mysofa fails to initialize, but it will
also handle allocation failure exceptions more gracefully here by just
returning a nullptr.

Ref #88

author: Julius Künzel
message:
Fix PkgConfig file for MSVC

m does not exist on MSVC, z is called zlib

author: Marvin Scholz
message:
Reword deprecation messages

The fact that the class is deprecated is already implied by the
presence of the attribute itself.

author: Marvin Scholz
message:
Use SPAUDIO_DEPRECATED instead of the C++ attribute

Fixes compilation with GCC 12 and older.

author: Marvin Scholz
message:
SpatialaudioAPI: Add SPAUDIO_DEPRECATED macro

This is a helper to declare an API as deprecated, adding a workaround
for GCC <13, which chokes when the native C++ attribute style [[]] is
combined with __attribute__(()).

author: Marvin Scholz
message:
Throw when unable to initialize mysofa

Do not silently create a unusable SOFA_HRTF object, instead throw an
exception when mysofa did not initialize properly, like when provided
with no or a unusable/bogus HRTF file.

That way the caller can properly handle this error condition instead of
crashing later due to the nullptr.

While this technically breaks the API contract, as this did not throw
before, this is clearly a better outcome than not handling the error.

author: Steve Lhomme
message: clean include formatting

author: Steve Lhomme
message:
Do not include math.h from C++ headers

We already include <cmath>

author: Steve Lhomme
message:
Define _USE_MATH_DEFINES when using libspatialaudio includes

Because we don't control the order of includes and we use M_PI/M_PI_2 in public
headers we need to make sure including <cmath> will provide the proper defines.

author: Steve Lhomme
message:
Always define _USE_MATH_DEFINES globally when building libspatialaudio

Depending on the order of includes math.h may be included before we have a chance to set the define

author: Steve Lhomme
message: Only define _USE_MATH_DEFINES if it's not defined

author: Steve Lhomme
message:
Use standard C++ abs() variant

Not to be confused with the C one that requires math.h.

message: CMake: add an option to enable/disable tests

author: Steve Lhomme
message: meson: fix missing CMAKE_PROJECT_VERSION values

author: Steve Lhomme
message:
CI: switch macos-13 to macos-15-intel

macos-13 is not available anymore. It was an Intel image [^1].
macos-13-xlarge being the arm64 one.

The oldest Intel image currently available is macos-14-large.
However the "-large" images seem to not be available unlimited for free.
We need to use macos-15-intel to free image targeting macOS Intel [^2].

Any macOS runner should be able to compile the code for both and probably run
tests for both architectures as well.

[^1]: https://github.com/actions/runner-images/commit/e664a5c3f32e9212d37658d11e258b1f63e99d45
[^2]: https://github.com/actions/runner-images/blob/c218bde724e0ae9730d9ce101bca654a4c2e521d/README.md

author: Peter Stitt
message: GainInterp: Fix bug that avoids processing the wrong channels. Should exclude channels of essentially zero gain

author: Marvin Scholz
message:
LoudspeakerLayouts: Exceptions can not be ignored

When an exception is thrown, there is no point providing a fallback
behavior. Even with -fno-exceptions it would just abort when trying
to unwind.

author: Marvin Scholz
message:
Renderer: Clarify error messages

Do not mention the old class name and change it to an Error in the
wording, as this should only ever happen when the API is used
incorrectly.
